title: Assess an existing CBC design
summary: Computes all valuation criteria (balance, D-efficiency) for a previously generated or externally constructed CBC design.

arguments.name: design_path
arguments.description: Path to the wide-format design CSV. Must have columns Version, Task, Concept followed by one column per attribute containing integer level codes.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Workspace directory where assessment artifacts will be written.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Run identifier; output files are written to {run_dir}/ocinfo/{run_name}_design/.

value: Produces a unified assessment with both BO (balance / overlap) and DE (D-efficiency) metrics for any design, regardless of the objective used to generate it. This allows comparing designs across objectives on common ground.
notes: The spec (attribute names, max levels) is inferred from the CSV header and data.
BO metrics include SSE of total and per-version marginals and pair counts.
DE metrics assume uniform 1/C choice probabilities (no beta prior) and effects coding with ridge=1e-6.

see_also.description: Balance / minimum-overlap design generator; faster and prior-free.
D-efficient design generator; useful when prior beliefs about respondent utilities exist.

artifacts.title: Design quality overview
artifacts.description: Balance, overlap, and D-efficiency metrics for the assessed design. Use these to compare competing design options objectively before fielding.

artifacts.title: Attribute level frequency counts
artifacts.description: How often each attribute level appears across the design. Roughly equal counts indicate a balanced design.

description:
Run `assess-design` on any CBC design file — whether generated by `design-bo`, `design-de`, or produced by an external tool — to compute a unified set of quality metrics. The assessment covers both BO-style balance/overlap criteria and the D-efficiency of the information matrix, so you can compare designs generated by different objectives on a common scale.

Use this endpoint as the quality-check step before fielding. Acceptable thresholds depend on the research design, but a starting point is: attribute-level counts within ±10% of the expected value for balance, and D-efficiency above 0.9 for reasonable statistical precision.
details:
The assessment computes both BO (balance/overlap) and D-efficiency metrics from the design CSV, regardless of which objective was used to generate it. Balance metrics measure how evenly each attribute level appears across tasks and versions — a perfectly balanced design has equal counts for every level of every attribute. Overlap measures how often the same attribute level appears more than once in the same task — lower overlap means respondents see more varied profiles, which generally improves information content. The SSE terms aggregate the per-level deviations from expected counts into a single quality number.

D-efficiency is the per-parameter geometric mean of the information matrix eigenvalues: `exp(log|M| / n_parameters)`. It is computed assuming uniform 1/C choice probabilities (no prior betas) and effects coding with a ridge of 1e-6. Values range from 0 to 1; a score of 1.0 means the design achieves the theoretical maximum information for its size.
